DifficultyNumber to BeatExplanation
Routine20Normal actions almost anyone can do without thinking, such as going up stairs two at a time or hearing a loud noise. Usually no check is required unless circumstances are unusual.
Easy40Common tasks most people can do, like remembering a song. Even someone inexperienced can usually succeed within a few attempts.
Moderate80Tasks requiring some knowledge or experience, such as picking a simple lock or curing a mild illness.
Difficult120Challenging tasks for an ordinary person, requiring significant skill or luck, such as scaling a wall unaided or performing advanced juggling.
Very Difficult140The upper limit of what a normal person can achieve, such as walking a high-wire or performing dangerous trapeze stunts.
Absurd180Only the exceptionally gifted or elite can perform these tasks reliably, such as analyzing every possible chess move instantly.
Almost Impossible240Even the best in the world usually fail at these tasks. An example might be catching a fly with chopsticks while blindfolded.
Impossible280Actions at the edge of physical reality—miraculous but still theoretically possible, such as dodging a bullet by calculating its trajectory.
Inhuman320Feats beyond logic, such as running 100 yards in two seconds or scoring a 17 on 18 holes of golf. Requires access to Inhuman Actions.
Zen440Completely beyond reality, such as riding a beam of light. Requires supernatural capability; ability alone is insufficient.
